data.content: The csproj doesn't enable implicit usings. I need to add the `using System.Collections.Generic;` etc. to the source, but since it's not my code to modify, let me just enable implicit usings in the project:

result.detailedContent:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-8c0f66bd29bc484b8d7421cdc67412ba/src/Contoso.Services.csproj b/tmp/sv-8c0f66bd29bc484b8d7421cdc67412ba/src/Contoso.Services.csproj
index 0000000..0000000 100644
--- a/tmp/sv-8c0f66bd29bc484b8d7421cdc67412ba/src/Contoso.Services.csproj
+++ b/tmp/sv-8c0f66bd29bc484b8d7421cdc67412ba/src/Contoso.Services.csproj
@@ -1,6 +1,7 @@
 <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
   <PropertyGroup>
     <TargetFramework>net9.0</TargetFramework>
+    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
   </PropertyGroup>
 </Project>

data.content: All 7 tests pass. Here's the final test file:

All **7 tests pass**. Each test uses the most appropriate MSTest assertion:

| Test | Assertion | Why |
|------|-----------|-----|
| Registration | `IsInstanceOfType<T>` | Verifies resolved type correctness |
| Same instance | `AreSame` | Reference equality for singleton check |
| Unregistered | `IsNull` | Confirms null return |
| Clear | `AreEqual(0, count)` | Verifies collection emptied |
| Remove | `IsNull` | Confirms service gone |
| Null registration | `ThrowsException<T>` | Guards against null input |
| ResolveRequired | `ThrowsException<T>` | Validates exception on missing service |

I also had to enable `ImplicitUsings` in both `.csproj` files since the source code uses `Dictionary<,>`, `Type`, etc. without explicit `using System.Collections.Generic;` directives.
